titleStmt.title: Letter from Kate English to her son, Patrick, 23 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Kate English

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Kate English (b. 1855) to her son, Patrick (1894-1970). Kate writes of receiving his letter and how happy she was to get it. She mentions that Patrick's cousin, Mickie, is serving at the front and was surprised when he heard of the trouble. She writes him to let the officer in charge of him that he has six cousins who are fighting in France, and lists them.This letter is part of a correspondence shared between Kate and Patrick during his imprisonment in Stafford and later Frongoch.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dunsink Cottages Castleknock 23rd May 1916 My Dear Patrick I received your
letter on Monday 22 May I
was delighted when I got it
as I was anxious to hear from
for 3
letters and I got no reply
perhaps you did not receive
them. I am sending you a
parcel it contains a cake
shirt socks tie brush 7 books
collar and and tie towel & soap
Beads if you are allowed
to get tea or sugar or
anything you want let me
know or will you be let
take money. let me know
in your next letter I am keeping strong myself
I hope you are the same
dont be fretting as you
will be a man yet. you
are but a child. Keep up
your poor heart as we
are all praying for you.
We had a letter from Mickie
at the front he has got
promotion. he was surprised
when he heard of the
trouble. Tell the officers in charge
of you that you have 6
first cousins fighting in
France. Michael English Christy Fenlon Annie's Husband
thats Joe O'Mahony Brigid Reillys
Husband. Sergeant Kelly and
the 2 Reillys of the high
Road. Lord Wimborne is
leaving Ireland and Nellie
& Frank expects to be going
away with them. Jamesy
David Katie Nellie and
all the rest of us join
sending you our best love
hoping to hear from you
soon again. my eyes will
be strained watching an
answer to know if you
got the parcel all right
GoodâBye. My Dear
Son Patrick and God
Bless you. GoodâBye x x x x x x
x x x x x x
I remain
Your Ever Loving Widow Mother
Mrs K. English
